page: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the AMD EPYC Genoa processor architecture. It features 12 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 23.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. The underlying hypervisor is QEMU. Storage is provided via a local 480 GB SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3041.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1947.0, both placing in the top 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 220.5 GB/sec for read-only operations and 143.81 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core performance falls into the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 10098.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 36626.57, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. These instructions assist in tasks like low-latency LLM inference; for example, prompt processing on a small 135M model achieves 2446.6 tokens/sec. However, due to the shared CPU allocation and a relatively low memory-to-vCPU ratio, the server is less suited for memory-heavy databases or sustained, non-burstable multi-threaded workloads. It is highly cost-efficient for batch processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference where dedicated CPU cores are not strictly required.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing AMD EPYC Genoa processors under a QEMU hypervisor. It delivers 12 shared vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 23.0 GB of RAM (1.95 GB per core), and 480 GB of local SSD storage, with no integrated GPU. The instance exhibits strong single-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench single-core (1947.0) and large-block memory read/write operations. However, its multi-core metrics, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 10098.0, remain in the average tier due to its shared CPU architecture. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets, the cpx52 is well-suited for workloads like lightweight LLM inference, static web serving, and parallelized text or image processing where high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency are prioritized over dedicated CPU guarantees.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 23.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is a QEMU-based virtual server with 12 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 23.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors, local SSD storage, and strong single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
23.0 GB of system memory and 480 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Built-in support for AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets
Suitable for lightweight LLM inference, web serving, and batch processing
